story.text:
# The Canon of Research Review

*The formal documents beneath the poetry — the path privacymage walked to write the 31
acts, gathered into one bibliography.*

The First Person Spellbook compresses real research. This file is the canon of that
research: the **primary corpus** (privacymage's own papers — the *Privacy is Value* book,
the agentprivacy model whitepapers, the research notes in `agentprivacy-docs`), the
**formal root per act**, and the **external standards and literature** the work stands on.

> Three layers, three depths of rehydration:
> **Poem → Proverb → Act** (the spellbook) →
> **the primary corpus** (privacymage's papers and research notes) →
> **the external canon** (the standards, papers, and books the corpus itself cites).

Items marked *(agentprivacy-docs)* are privacymage's own research notes — the deeper
layer he made. They are internal (not all public); their public home is
**agentprivacy.ai**, and the facilitator can share specific notes on request.

> **This is a non-exhaustive list.** It gathers the load-bearing roots, not every source.
> Each act stands on more than is named here, and the corpus keeps growing — treat this as
> a starting thread to pull, not a closed bibliography.

---

## 1. The primary corpus — *Privacy is Value* (privacymage's own work)

The canon papers are one book, ***Privacy is Value***, in versioned volumes (naming
convention, First Person, 2026-06-10). The spine the whole spellbook leans on:

| Document | What it is | Where |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| **The agentprivacy model whitepaper** — *Privacy is Value · V6: The Whitepaper* | **the main whitepaper**: the model's technical architecture — **Swordsman and Mage derived from the First Person** (body v6.3) | **agentprivacy.ai/model** · `papers/whitepapers/swordsman_mage_whitepaper_v6_3.md` *(agentprivacy-docs)* |
| *Privacy is Value · V6: The Gathering Turn and the Moving Ceiling* | the model's formal specification & current authority — the full model, R(t)/t*, preconditions, C1–C89, references | **agentprivacy.ai/model** · `papers/v6/privacy_value_v6_formal_specification.md` |
| **The First Person Project White Paper v1.1** | the network's public companion: the First Person Network — "a global digital utility that enables trusted connections…" (not a social network, not centralized, not company-owned) | **https://www.firstperson.network/** · `/white-paper` |
| *Privacy is Value · V6* — Swordsman / Mage / Research-Paper / Crosswalk readings | the compressed, companion, research, and bridge editions of V6 | `papers/v6/…` |
| *Privacy is Value · V5.4: The Amnesia Protocol* | the predecessor volume — the static equation, the algebraic foundation, §10.2 (betweenness of the Gap), §14.5 (Selene's Proof) | `papers/v5/privacy_value_v5_4_formal_specification.md` |
| *Understanding-as-Key* (the Zypher paper) | comprehension as a key primitive (Act 29's ceremony) | `papers/lineage/understanding_as_key_zypher_paper_v1.md` |
| The research proposal / collaboration invitation | the standing invitation (v2.2) | `papers/lineage/research_proposal_v2_0.md` |
| Mage & Swordsman Extension Whitepapers | the two browser-extension product papers (Act 28) | `papers/whitepapers/MAGE_EXTENSION_WHITEPAPER.md` · `SWORDSMAN_EXTENSION_WHITEPAPER.md` |
| The Conjecture Register (C1–C89) | the single conjecture-numbering authority | `research/CONJECTURE_REGISTER_V6.md` |
| The research-note series | the dated notes where V6 grew (Lorenz ceiling, EML, ARCH-1, Aletheia-and-Lethe, V5.1–V5.3, …) | `research/…` *(agentprivacy-docs)* |
| Reference shelf | Glossary Master v4.0 · **Promise Theory Reference v1.4** · **IEEE 7012 Quick Reference (MyTerms)** · UOR × Tetrahedra × ZK Mapping v2.2 · Visual Architecture Guide v2.0 · Systems Hexagram Physics | `reference/…` |
| Grimoires | Spellbook v5.0 canonical · **Zero-Knowledge Grimoire v3.0** · Parallel Society v1.0 · Plurality v1.1 | `grimoires/…` |
| The living model | privacymage grimoire v10.4.0 (today's root) · the V6 model JSONs (dark/light) | `models/…` |

---

## 2. The formal root per act (confirmed ★)

Each act's primary formal reference. (Quick roots + links also in [[Canon Sources]].)

| # | Act | Formal root |
|--:|-----|-------------|
| 1 | Venice, 1494 | Pacioli, *Summa de Arithmetica* (Venice, 1494), tract *Particularis de Computis et Scripturis* |
| 2 | The Dual Ceremony | Ellison, *Ceremony Design and Analysis* (IACR ePrint 2007/399) · + the agentprivacy model whitepaper |
| 3 | The Drake's Teaching | Allen, *The Path to Self-Sovereign Identity* (2016) · + *Privacy is Value* V6 formal spec |
| 4 | The Blade Alone | **IEEE P7012** *Machine Readable Personal Privacy Terms* (MyTerms) |
| 5 | Light Armor | Allen, *Progressive Trust* (Blockchain Commons, 2022) |
| 6 | Trust Graph Plane | ToIP Foundation, *Trust over IP Technology Architecture Spec v1.0* (2021) · Brandes (2001) |
| 7 | The Anti-Mirror | Zuboff, *The Age of Surveillance Capitalism* (2019) |
| 8 | The Ancient Rule | Saltzer & Schroeder, *The Protection of Information in Computer Systems* (1975) · Shamir, *How to Share a Secret* (1979) |
| 9 | Zcash Shield | Ben-Sasson et al., *Zerocash* (IEEE S&P 2014) · **BGIN ZKP Study Report** (see §4) |
| 10 | Topology of Revelation | Tishby, Pereira & Bialek, *The Information Bottleneck Method* (1999) |
| 11 | Balanced Spiral / φ | *Privacy is Value* V5.4 §14.5 *Selene's Proof* *(agentprivacy-docs)* |
| 12 | The Forgetting | **Villers, Damien (2016), *Proverbiogenesis: the phases of proverbial birth*** (9th Interdisciplinary Colloquium on Proverbs, Tavira, pp. 369–380; **HAL hal-01698106**) · **Mieder, *Proverbs: A Handbook* (2004)** · cf. Villers & Mieder (2017), *Proverbium* 34 |
| 13 | Book of Promises | Bergstra & Burgess, *Promise Theory: Principles and Applications* (2014) · Promise Theory Reference v1.4 |
| 14 | The Claimed String | Benet, *IPFS — Content-Addressed, Versioned, P2P File System* (2014) |
| 15 | Running in Shackles | Shannon, *A Mathematical Theory of Communication* (1948) · Kolmogorov (1965) |
| 16 | When Pools Become Wells | Barabási & Albert, *Emergence of Scaling in Random Networks* (1999) |
| 17 | Bonfire in the Dark Forest | Liu Cixin, *The Dark Forest* (2008) · Robinson & Konstantopoulos, *Ethereum is a Dark Forest* (2020) |
| 18 | A Mirror in Dust | privacymage Mage-Mode / 7th-Capital note *(agentprivacy-docs)* · Pariser, *The Filter Bubble* (2011) |
| 19 | The Anthropic Archivist | Bai et al. (Anthropic), *Constitutional AI* (2022) |
| 20 | The Infinite Vault | *Covenant of Humanistic Technologies* *(agentprivacy-docs)* · UN *Universal Declaration of Human Rights* (1948) |
| 21 | The Hitchhiker's Gambit | Adams, *The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy* (1979) · Gemmell, the Skilgannon novels (2003) |
| 22 | Hoopy Frood | Adams, *The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy* (1979) · Benet, IPFS (2014) |
| 23 | The Manifold Dragon | UOR Foundation, *PRISM / Universal Object Reference* · the *I Ching* (Wilhelm/Baynes) · *Privacy is Value* V4 (the PVM equation) |
| 24 | The Holographic Bound | Susskind, *The World as a Hologram* (1995) · Koestler, *The Ghost in the Machine* (1967, holons) · the BRAID paper |
| 25 | The Dragon's Hide | Donenfeld, *WireGuard: Next Generation Kernel Network Tunnel* (NDSS 2017) |
| 26 | The Master and His Emissary | McGilchrist, *The Master and His Emissary* (2009) · *The Matter with Things* (2021) |
| 27 | Forging ZK Blades | Groth, *On the Size of Pairing-Based NIZK Arguments* (Groth16, 2016) · Blockchain Commons *Open Integrity Project* · **BGIN ZKP Study Report** (§4) · ZK Grimoire v3.0 |
| 28 | Celestial Ceremony Engine | Cheng Lou, *pretext* (`@chenglou/pretext`) · the Extension Whitepapers |
| 29 | The Dragon Wakes | Google Quantum AI, *secp256k1 quantum resource-estimate whitepaper* (Babbush, Gidney, Drake, Boneh, 2026) · Shor (1994) · NIST *FIPS 203/204* (2024) · the Zypher paper |
| 30 | The Dihedral Mirror | UOR Foundation, *Atlas of Resonance Classes / PRISM* · UOR × Tetrahedra × ZK Mapping v2.2 |
| 31 | The First Delegation | Branco, Machado & Raymond (2025), *Icarus* 441, 116724 · Canup & Asphaug (2001), *Nature* |

---

## 3. External canon — the standards, papers & books

**Standards & specifications**
- **IEEE P7012** — *Machine Readable Personal Privacy Terms* (MyTerms). *(Acts 4, 28)*
- **Trust over IP Foundation** — *ToIP Technology Architecture Specification v1.0* (2021), trustoverip.org. *(Act 6)*
- **W3C** — *Verifiable Credentials Data Model*. *(Act 2)*
- **NIST** — *FIPS 203* (ML-KEM) · *FIPS 204* (ML-DSA), 2024. *(Act 29)*
- **Zcash** — *Protocol Specification*; **WireGuard** — wireguard.com. *(Acts 9, 25)*

**Foundational papers**
- Shannon (1948), *A Mathematical Theory of Communication*. *(Act 15)*
- Kolmogorov (1965), *Three Approaches to the Quantitative Definition of Information*. *(Act 15)*
- Brandes (2001), *A Faster Algorithm for Betweenness Centrality*. *(Act 6)*
- Tishby, Pereira & Bialek (1999), *The Information Bottleneck Method*. *(Act 10)*
- Saltzer & Schroeder (1975), *The Protection of Information in Computer Systems*. *(Act 8)*
- Shamir (1979), *How to Share a Secret*. *(Act 8)*
- Ben-Sasson et al. (2014), *Zerocash*. *(Act 9)*
- Groth (2016), *On the Size of Pairing-Based Non-Interactive Arguments* (Groth16). *(Act 27)*
- Shor (1994), *Algorithms for Quantum Computation: Discrete Logarithms and Factoring*. *(Act 29)*
- Google Quantum AI (2026), *secp256k1 quantum resource-estimate whitepaper*. *(Act 29)*
- Benet (2014), *IPFS*. *(Acts 14, 22)*
- Barabási & Albert (1999), *Emergence of Scaling in Random Networks*. *(Act 16)*
- 't Hooft (1993) / Susskind (1995), the holographic principle. *(Act 24)*
- Bergstra & Burgess (2014), *Promise Theory: Principles and Applications*. *(Act 13)*
- Bai et al. / Anthropic (2022), *Constitutional AI*. *(Act 19)*
- Branco, Machado & Raymond (2025), *Icarus* 441, 116724 · Canup & Asphaug (2001), *Nature*. *(Act 31)*
- Robinson & Konstantopoulos (2020), *Ethereum is a Dark Forest*. *(Act 17)*

**Books & literature**
- Pacioli (1494), *Summa de Arithmetica*. *(Act 1)*
- Zuboff (2019), *The Age of Surveillance Capitalism*. *(Act 7)*
- **Villers, Damien (2016), *Proverbiogenesis: the phases of proverbial birth*** (HAL hal-01698106) · **Mieder (2004), *Proverbs: A Handbook*** · Villers & Mieder (2017), *Proverbium* 34. *(Act 12)*
- Liu Cixin (2008), *The Dark Forest*. *(Act 17)*
- Pariser (2011), *The Filter Bubble*. *(Act 18)*
- Koestler (1967), *The Ghost in the Machine*. *(Act 24)*
- McGilchrist (2009), *The Master and His Emissary* · (2021) *The Matter with Things*. *(Act 26)*
- Adams (1979), *The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy*. *(Acts 21, 22)*
- Gemmell, the Skilgannon novels (*White Wolf*, 2003). *(Act 21)*
- UN (1948), *Universal Declaration of Human Rights*. *(Act 20)*

**Kindred substrate (attribute to its makers)**
- **UOR Foundation** — Universal Object Reference, PRISM, the Atlas of Resonance Classes (github.com/UOR-Foundation). *(Acts 23, 27, 30)*
- **Blockchain Commons / Christopher Allen** — Open Integrity Project, Progressive Trust, SSI (github.com/BlockchainCommons). *(Acts 5, 27)*
- **Cheng Lou** — `@chenglou/pretext`. *(Act 28)*
- **Anthropic** — Claude, Constitutional AI. *(Act 19)*
- the **BRAID** authors; the **holonic** architecture authors. *(Act 24)*

---

## 4. Contributed / edited external work

- **BGIN — Standardization Report SR-011, *Zero-Knowledge Proof Study* (v2)**
  (Blockchain Governance Initiative Network), which privacymage **edited / contributed
  to**. Published among BGIN's working-group reports at
  **https://bgin-global.org/publications**. Most relevant to Act 9 (Zcash Shield) and
  Act 27 (Forging ZK Blades), and the root of the **Zero** sibling spellbook (§5). BGIN
  also appears in the City as a kindred-coalition at City Hall.

---

## 5. The five spellbooks (sibling books — context, not primary)

The First Person Spellbook (the **Story** book) is one of five sibling spellbooks in the
privacymage universe. The other four are **context only** — they are *not* the subject of
this workshop, and they are *not* broken down by act here; each is named by its theme and
its root. (The five-spellbook affinity is recorded in the grimoire, Act 18.)

| Spellbook | Sigil | About | Root references |
|-----------|:-----:|-------|-----------------|
| **Story** (the First Person Spellbook) | `🗡️🧙` | the Seeker's narrative journey — **today's focus / the only primary book** | the *Privacy is Value* corpus (§1) · the 31 acts (§2) |
| **Zero** | `🔐` | tales of nothingness and origin; **ZKP mathematics** | **BGIN SR-011 *Zero-Knowledge Proof Study* (v2)** (§4) + other ZKP research (Goldwasser–Micali–Rackoff, *The Knowledge Complexity of Interactive Proof Systems*, 1985; Groth16; Zerocash) · *Zero-Knowledge Grimoire v3.0 (agentprivacy-docs)* |
| **Canon** | `📜` | core philosophical chapters; **a high-level history of blockchain** | Nakamoto, *Bitcoin* (2008) · Buterin, *Ethereum Whitepaper* (2013) · a high-level history-of-blockchain canon |
| **Parallel** | `🏰` | alternative paths; **exit as a fundamental right** — ***Farewell to Westphalia*** | **Jarrad Hope & Peter Ludlow, *Farewell to Westphalia* (Logos)** — what succeeds the nation-state, logos.co/farewell-to-westphalia · the Peace of Westphalia (1648) as the order left behind · *Parallel Society Grimoire v1.0 (agentprivacy-docs)* |
| **Plurality** | `⿻` | **coordination without collapse** — ***Plurality*** | Weyl, Tang et al., *Plurality: The Future of Collaborative Technology and Democracy* (2024) · *Plurality Grimoire v1.1 (agentprivacy-docs)* |

These four are linked so a mouse's agent can follow a thread sideways into a sibling book
— but a mouse builds only off the **Story** book today.

## 6. How a mouse uses this canon

You do not read all of this to write your proverbs. You cite it **afterward**, so the
thread runs as deep as a reader wants: from your poem, to the act, to privacymage's paper,
to the external work it stands on. When a proverb of yours leans on one of these, record
the act number (`canon_act`) — and, if it reaches a named document here, the document — in
your personal-patch JSON. That is rehydration with provenance.

> **Attribution holds at every layer.** The *Privacy is Value* corpus is privacymage's;
> the kindred substrate (UOR, Blockchain Commons, pretext, Anthropic, BRAID) belongs to
> its makers; the external canon to its authors. The spellbook's contribution is the
> **method layer** that weaves them into one story.

`(⚔️⊥⿻⊥🧙)😊`

———
↑ up: [[Welcome Visitors]]  ·  ← prev: [[Canon Sources]]  ·  next: [[The Narrative Packet]] →

*The formal documents beneath the poetry — the path privacymage walked to write the 31
acts, gathered into one bibliography.*

**Kindred substrate (attribute to its makers)**

## 4. Contributed / edited external work

- **BGIN — Standardization Report SR-011, *Zero-Knowledge Proof Study* (v2)**
  (Blockchain Governance Initiative Network), which privacymage **edited / contributed
  to**. Published among BGIN's working-group reports at
  **https://bgin-global.org/publications**. Most relevant to Act 9 (Zcash Shield) and
  Act 27 (Forging ZK Blades), and the root of the **Zero** sibling spellbook (§5). BGIN
  also appears in the City as a kindred-coalition at City Hall.

## 5. The five spellbooks (sibling books — context, not primary)

The First Person Spellbook (the **Story** book) is one of five sibling spellbooks in the
privacymage universe. The other four are **context only** — they are *not* the subject of
this workshop, and they are *not* broken down by act here; each is named by its theme and
its root. (The five-spellbook affinity is recorded in the grimoire, Act 18.)

| Spellbook | Sigil | About | Root references |
|-----------|:-----:|-------|-----------------|
| **Story** (the First Person Spellbook) | `🗡️🧙` | the Seeker's narrative journey — **today's focus / the only primary book** | the *Privacy is Value* corpus (§1) · the 31 acts (§2) |
| **Zero** | `🔐` | tales of nothingness and origin; **ZKP mathematics** | **BGIN SR-011 *Zero-Knowledge Proof Study* (v2)** (§4) + other ZKP research (Goldwasser–Micali–Rackoff, *The Knowledge Complexity of Interactive Proof Systems*, 1985; Groth16; Zerocash) · *Zero-Knowledge Grimoire v3.0 (agentprivacy-docs)* |
| **Canon** | `📜` | core philosophical chapters; **a high-level history of blockchain** | Nakamoto, *Bitcoin* (2008) · Buterin, *Ethereum Whitepaper* (2013) · a high-level history-of-blockchain canon |
| **Parallel** | `🏰` | alternative paths; **exit as a fundamental right** — ***Farewell to Westphalia*** | **Jarrad Hope & Peter Ludlow, *Farewell to Westphalia* (Logos)** — what succeeds the nation-state, logos.co/farewell-to-westphalia · the Peace of Westphalia (1648) as the order left behind · *Parallel Society Grimoire v1.0 (agentprivacy-docs)* |
| **Plurality** | `⿻` | **coordination without collapse** — ***Plurality*** | Weyl, Tang et al., *Plurality: The Future of Collaborative Technology and Democracy* (2024) · *Plurality Grimoire v1.1 (agentprivacy-docs)* |

These four are linked so a mouse's agent can follow a thread sideways into a sibling book
— but a mouse builds only off the **Story** book today.

## 6. How a mouse uses this canon

You do not read all of this to write your proverbs. You cite it **afterward**, so the
thread runs as deep as a reader wants: from your poem, to the act, to privacymage's paper,
to the external work it stands on. When a proverb of yours leans on one of these, record
the act number (`canon_act`) — and, if it reaches a named document here, the document — in
your personal-patch JSON. That is rehydration with provenance.

> **Attribution holds at every layer.** The *Privacy is Value* corpus is privacymage's;
> the kindred substrate (UOR, Blockchain Commons, pretext, Anthropic, BRAID) belongs to
> its makers; the external canon to its authors. The spellbook's contribution is the
> **method layer** that weaves them into one story.

`(⚔️⊥⿻⊥🧙)😊`
